Class Enemy

java.lang.Object
generation.characters.Character
generation.characters.Enemy
Direct Known Subclasses:
Ghost, Hunter
public class Enemy extends Character
Represents a basic enemy (Zombie) that moves randomly each turn. Displayed as R on the map.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from class Character

    initialPosition, lives, name, position
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected int[]
    initialPosition
    Spawn position used when resetting after a collision or trap.
    protected int
    lives
    Number of lives remaining.
    protected String
    name
    Display name of this character.
    protected int[]
    position
    Current grid position {row, col}.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Movement
    chooseMovement()
    Chooses a random direction (UP, DOWN, LEFT, or RIGHT — never NONE).

    Method
    Description
    int[]

    int

    String

    Gets the character's name.
    int[]
    getPosition()
    Gets the current grid position.
    void
    move(Movement move, Cell[][] map)
    Moves the character in the given direction, wrapping around map edges.
    void
    setInitialPosition(int[] initialPosition)
    Sets the initial spawn position used when resetting.
    void
    setLives(int lives)
    Sets the number of lives, clamping negative values to 0.
    void
    setPosition(int[] position)
    Sets the current position.

  • Constructor Details

    • Enemy

      public Enemy(String name, int lives, int[] position)
      Constructs a new Enemy with the given name, lives, and starting position.
      Parameters:
      name - The enemy's name.
      lives - The starting number of lives.
      position - The initial grid coordinates {row, col}.

  • Method Details

    • chooseMovement

      public Movement chooseMovement()
      Chooses a random direction (UP, DOWN, LEFT, or RIGHT — never NONE).
      Specified by:
      chooseMovement in class Character
      Returns:
      A randomly selected Movement.
